In [3]:
# %% [markdown]
# **Files (same folder as this script):**
# - `country_demographics.csv`
# - `Engagement.csv`
# - `Retention.csv`
# - `User_Behavior.csv`
#
# **How to use in Jupyter:** Copy each `# %%` block into its own cell, or open this `.py` file in Jupyter/VS Code and run cell-by-cell.

# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 0 — SETUP (paths, libraries, display options)
# =============================================================================
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns

# Folder where the 4 CSV files live (same folder as this file / your notebook)
try:
    DATA_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent  # when running as .py
except NameError:
    DATA_DIR = Path(".")  # Jupyter: set notebook working directory to cleaned/
# If paths fail, uncomment and set full path:
# DATA_DIR = Path(r"d:\Data Analytics\Datasets\Datasets\cleaned")

# Figures saved next to the CSVs (creates folder if missing)
FIG_DIR = DATA_DIR / "figures"
FIG_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# Analysis period start (Firebase export is Jan–Dec 2025)
START_DATE = pd.Timestamp("2025-01-01")

sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid", context="notebook")
plt.rcParams["figure.figsize"] = (10, 4)
In [4]:
# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 1 — LOAD ALL 4 CLEANED CSV FILES
# =============================================================================
# Each file is one table on disk but contains several "mini-reports"
# identified by the column `section` (or `table` in Retention.csv).

geo = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "country_demographics.csv")
eng = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "Engagement.csv")
ret = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "Retention.csv")
beh = pd.read_csv(DATA_DIR / "User_Behavior.csv")

print("Loaded rows:")
print(f"  country_demographics: {len(geo):,}")
print(f"  Engagement:           {len(eng):,}")
print(f"  Retention:            {len(ret):,}")
print(f"  User_Behavior:        {len(beh):,}")

Loaded rows:
  country_demographics: 92
  Engagement:           848
  Retention:            987
  User_Behavior:        1,916
In [5]:
# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 2 — PREPROCESS: country_demographics.csv
# =============================================================================
# Grain: one row per country (full-year snapshot).
# No `section` column — use the whole dataframe.

geo = geo.copy()
geo["country"] = geo["country"].astype(str).str.strip()

# Derived KPIs for behaviour analysis (ignore total_revenue if behaviour-only)
geo["events_per_user"] = geo["event_count"] / geo["active_users"]
geo["key_events_per_user"] = geo["key_events"] / geo["active_users"]
geo["low_sample"] = geo["active_users"] < 5  # flag small countries in charts

print("Top 5 countries by active users:")
print(geo.nlargest(5, "active_users")[["country", "active_users", "engagement_rate"]])

Top 5 countries by active users:
          country  active_users  engagement_rate
0   United States        1106.0           0.8621
1       Australia          89.0           0.8231
2          Canada          77.0           0.8126
3  United Kingdom          61.0           0.8246
4     Netherlands          48.0           0.8603
In [6]:
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 3 — PREPROCESS: Engagement.csv (filter by `section`)
# =============================================================================
# IMPORTANT: Never analyse the full `eng` dataframe in one chart — grains differ.

eng_weekly = eng[eng["section"] == "weekly_users"].copy()
eng_daily = eng[eng["section"] == "daily_active_users"].copy()
eng_ratios = eng[eng["section"] == "daily_user_ratios"].copy()
eng_events = eng[eng["section"] == "event_count"].copy()
eng_audience = eng[eng["section"] == "audience"].copy()
eng_weekly_eng = eng[eng["section"] == "weekly_engagement"].copy()

# Integer day/week keys for joins and plots
eng_daily["day_number"] = pd.to_numeric(eng_daily["day_number"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
eng_ratios["day_number"] = pd.to_numeric(eng_ratios["day_number"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
eng_weekly["week_number"] = pd.to_numeric(eng_weekly["week_number"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")

# Ratio columns exist on BOTH daily_active_users (empty) and daily_user_ratios (filled).
# Drop them from eng_daily BEFORE merge, or pandas creates dau_mau_ratio_x / _y.
RATIO_COLS = ["dau_mau_ratio", "dau_wau_ratio", "wau_mau_ratio"]

eng_daily_slim = eng_daily.drop(columns=RATIO_COLS, errors="ignore")

eng_daily_full = eng_daily_slim.merge(
    eng_ratios[["day_number"] + RATIO_COLS],
    on="day_number",
    how="left",
)

# Backup: compute DAU/MAU if ratios still missing
eng_daily_full["dau_mau_ratio"] = pd.to_numeric(eng_daily_full["dau_mau_ratio"], errors="coerce")
eng_daily_full["dau_mau_computed"] = (
    pd.to_numeric(eng_daily_full["dau_1d"], errors="coerce")
    / pd.to_numeric(eng_daily_full["mau_30d"], errors="coerce")
)
eng_daily_full["dau_mau_ratio"] = eng_daily_full["dau_mau_ratio"].fillna(
    eng_daily_full["dau_mau_computed"]
)

# Calendar date for nicer x-axis labels
eng_daily_full["calendar_date"] = START_DATE + pd.to_timedelta(
    eng_daily_full["day_number"].astype(float), unit="D"
)

# Helper for later sections (handles _x/_y if you ran an old merge earlier)
def stickiness_series(df):
    """Return DAU/MAU as a Series from eng_daily_full."""
    if "dau_mau_ratio" in df.columns and df["dau_mau_ratio"].notna().any():
        return pd.to_numeric(df["dau_mau_ratio"], errors="coerce")
    if "dau_mau_ratio_y" in df.columns:
        return pd.to_numeric(df["dau_mau_ratio_y"], errors="coerce")
    return pd.to_numeric(df["dau_1d"], errors="coerce") / pd.to_numeric(df["mau_30d"], errors="coerce")

# Event mix: each event as % of all events in the export
eng_events["event_count"] = pd.to_numeric(eng_events["event_count"], errors="coerce")
eng_events["event_pct"] = eng_events["event_count"] / eng_events["event_count"].sum() * 100

print("Engagement sections prepared:")
print(f"  weekly_users:      {len(eng_weekly)} rows")
print(f"  daily (merged):    {len(eng_daily_full)} rows")
print(f"  event_count:       {len(eng_events)} rows")
print("  Columns in eng_daily_full:", [c for c in eng_daily_full.columns if "dau" in c.lower() or "mau" in c.lower()])

Engagement sections prepared:
  weekly_users:      53 rows
  daily (merged):    365 rows
  event_count:       10 rows
  Columns in eng_daily_full: ['mau_30d', 'dau_1d', 'dau_mau_ratio', 'dau_wau_ratio', 'wau_mau_ratio', 'dau_mau_computed']
In [7]:
# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 4 — PREPROCESS: Retention.csv (filter by `table`)
# =============================================================================

ret_weekly = ret[ret["table"] == "weekly_users"].copy()
ret_cohort_ret = ret[ret["table"] == "cohort_day1_7_30_retention_rate"].copy()
ret_cohort_eng = ret[ret["table"] == "cohort_day1_7_30_avg_engagement_sec"].copy()

ret_weekly["week_number"] = pd.to_numeric(ret_weekly["week_number"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
ret_weekly["week_start_date"] = pd.to_datetime(ret_weekly["week_start_date"], errors="coerce")

ret_cohort_ret["cohort_day"] = pd.to_numeric(ret_cohort_ret["cohort_day"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
ret_cohort_ret["calendar_date"] = START_DATE + pd.to_timedelta(
    ret_cohort_ret["cohort_day"].astype(float), unit="D"
)

# returning_pct was added during CSV cleaning; recompute if column missing
if "returning_pct" not in ret_weekly.columns:
    ret_weekly["returning_pct"] = ret_weekly["returning_users"] / ret_weekly["total_users"]

# Day-30 retention is sparse — use Day-1 and Day-7 for main charts
d30_count = ret_cohort_ret["day_30_retention_rate"].notna().sum()
print(f"Note: only {d30_count} cohort rows have day_30_retention_rate (often too sparse for charts).")

# Merge weekly engagement + weekly retention on week_number (cross-file link)
weekly_merged = eng_weekly.merge(
    ret_weekly[["week_number", "new_users", "returning_users", "total_users", "returning_pct"]],
    on="week_number",
    how="inner",
    suffixes=("_eng", "_ret"),
)
print("Weekly merged rows (Engagement + Retention):", len(weekly_merged))

Note: only 19 cohort rows have day_30_retention_rate (often too sparse for charts).
Weekly merged rows (Engagement + Retention): 53
In [8]:
# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 5 — PREPROCESS: User_Behavior.csv (filter by `section`)
# =============================================================================

beh_overview = beh[beh["section"] == "overview"].copy()
beh_screens = beh[beh["section"] == "screen_views"].copy()
beh_daily_ret = beh[beh["section"] == "user_retention"].copy()
beh_audience = beh[beh["section"] == "audience"].copy()
beh_traffic = beh[beh["section"] == "traffic_source"].copy()
beh_platform = beh[beh["section"] == "platform"].copy()
beh_cities = beh[beh["section"] == "city"].copy()

beh_screens["event_count"] = pd.to_numeric(beh_screens["event_count"], errors="coerce")
beh_screens["bounce_rate"] = pd.to_numeric(beh_screens["bounce_rate"], errors="coerce")
beh_screens = beh_screens.sort_values("event_count", ascending=False)

beh_daily_ret["day_number"] = pd.to_numeric(beh_daily_ret["day_number"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
beh_daily_ret["returning_pct"] = beh_daily_ret["returning_users"] / beh_daily_ret["total_users"]
beh_daily_ret["calendar_date"] = START_DATE + pd.to_timedelta(
    beh_daily_ret["day_number"].astype(float), unit="D"
)

# Cities: most rows are 1 user — keep Top 25 for charts only
beh_cities["active_users"] = pd.to_numeric(beh_cities["active_users"], errors="coerce")
beh_cities_top = beh_cities.nlargest(25, "active_users")

print("User_Behavior sections prepared:")
print(f"  screen_views:   {len(beh_screens)} screens")
print(f"  user_retention: {len(beh_daily_ret)} days")
print(f"  cities (top25): {len(beh_cities_top)} for plotting")

User_Behavior sections prepared:
  screen_views:   12 screens
  user_retention: 365 days
  cities (top25): 25 for plotting
In [9]:
# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 6 — DATA QUALITY CHECKS (run once; paste results in report)
# =============================================================================

checks = {
    "eng_weekly rows (expect 53)": len(eng_weekly),
    "eng_daily rows (expect 365)": len(eng_daily_full),
    "ret_weekly rows (expect 53)": len(ret_weekly),
    "beh_daily_ret rows (expect 365)": len(beh_daily_ret),
    "countries (expect ~92)": len(geo),
    "duplicate countries": geo["country"].duplicated().sum(),
    "day_30 retention non-null": ret_cohort_ret["day_30_retention_rate"].notna().sum(),
}

# Week 0: small gap between Engagement active_users and Retention total_users is normal
w0_eng = eng_weekly.loc[eng_weekly["week_number"] == 0, "active_users"].squeeze()
w0_ret = ret_weekly.loc[ret_weekly["week_number"] == 0, "total_users"].squeeze()
checks["week 0 active_users (eng)"] = w0_eng
checks["week 0 total_users (ret)"] = w0_ret

for k, v in checks.items():
    print(f"  {k}: {v}")

  eng_weekly rows (expect 53): 53
  eng_daily rows (expect 365): 365
  ret_weekly rows (expect 53): 53
  beh_daily_ret rows (expect 365): 365
  countries (expect ~92): 92
  duplicate countries: 0
  day_30 retention non-null: 19
  week 0 active_users (eng): 37.0
  week 0 total_users (ret): 39.0

# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 7 — ANALYSIS: Geography (country_demographics)
# =============================================================================
# Business question: Where are players most engaged?

top_countries_users = geo.nlargest(10, "active_users")[
    ["country", "active_users", "new_users", "engagement_rate", "avg_engagement_time_sec"]
]
top_countries_engagement = geo[~geo["low_sample"]].nlargest(10, "engagement_rate")[
    ["country", "active_users", "engagement_rate", "events_per_user"]
]

print("\n--- Top 10 countries by active users ---")
print(top_countries_users.to_string(index=False))

print("\n--- Top 10 countries by engagement rate (excluding low_sample) ---")
print(top_countries_engagement.to_string(index=False))

In [11]:
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 8 — ANALYSIS: Engagement & growth (Engagement + weekly merge)
# =============================================================================
# Business question: How do DAU/MAU and weekly active users trend over 2025?
# Requires Section 3 to be run first (eng_daily_full, stickiness_series).

print("\n--- Weekly active users (first & last 3 weeks) ---")
print(eng_weekly[["week_number", "active_users", "new_users"]].head(3).to_string(index=False))
print("...")
print(eng_weekly[["week_number", "active_users", "new_users"]].tail(3).to_string(index=False))

print("\n--- Average stickiness (DAU/MAU) ---")
dau_mau = stickiness_series(eng_daily_full)
print(f"  Mean dau_mau_ratio: {dau_mau.mean():.3f}")
print(f"  Max dau_mau_ratio:  {dau_mau.max():.3f}")

print("\n--- Event mix (top 5 by count) ---")
print(
    eng_events.nlargest(5, "event_count")[["event_name", "event_count", "event_pct"]].to_string(
        index=False
    )
)

print("\n--- Audience segments ---")
print(beh_audience[["audience", "active_users"]].to_string(index=False))

# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 9 — ANALYSIS: Retention (Retention + User_Behavior daily)
# =============================================================================
# Business question: Do users come back? (aggregate retention, not player-level churn ML)

print("\n--- Weekly returning % (first & last 3 weeks) ---")
cols = ["week_number", "returning_users", "new_users", "total_users", "returning_pct"]
print(ret_weekly[cols].head(3).to_string(index=False))
print("...")
print(ret_weekly[cols].tail(3).to_string(index=False))

cohort_with_d7 = ret_cohort_ret.dropna(subset=["day_7_retention_rate"])
print("\n--- Cohort Day-7 retention (summary) ---")
print(cohort_with_d7["day_7_retention_rate"].describe().round(4))

print("\n--- Purchasers vs All Users (from User_Behavior) ---")
print(beh_audience[["audience", "active_users"]].to_string(index=False))

# %% =============================================================================
# SECTION 10 — ANALYSIS: In-app behaviour (screens & traffic)
# =============================================================================
# Business question: Which screens and flows matter?

print("\n--- Top screens by event_count ---")
print(
    beh_screens[["screen_class", "active_users", "event_count", "views", "bounce_rate"]]
    .head(8)
    .to_string(index=False)
)

print("\n--- Traffic source ---")
if len(beh_traffic) > 0:
    print(beh_traffic[["type", "source_medium", "active_users"]].to_string(index=False))

print("\n--- Platform ---")
if len(beh_platform) > 0:
    print(beh_platform[["platform"]].dropna().to_string(index=False))

In [14]:
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 12 — VISUALIZE: Country demographics (display in notebook)
# =============================================================================

# Chart 1 — Top 15 countries by active users
top15 = geo.nlargest(15, "active_users").sort_values("active_users")
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.barh(top15["country"], top15["active_users"], color="steelblue")
plt.xlabel("Active users")
plt.title("Top 15 countries by active users (2025)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Chart 2 — Engagement rate vs scale (scatter)
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
subset = geo[~geo["low_sample"]]
plt.scatter(subset["active_users"], subset["engagement_rate"], alpha=0.7)
plt.xlabel("Active users")
plt.ylabel("Engagement rate")
plt.title("Country engagement rate vs user base")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [15]:
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 13 — VISUALIZE: Engagement over time (display in notebook)
# =============================================================================

# Chart 3 — Weekly active & new users
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(eng_weekly["week_number"], eng_weekly["active_users"], label="Active users", marker="o", ms=3)
ax.plot(eng_weekly["week_number"], eng_weekly["new_users"], label="New users", marker="o", ms=3)
ax.set_xlabel("Week number")
ax.set_ylabel("Users")
ax.set_title("Weekly active and new users")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Chart 4 — Daily DAU / MAU / WAU
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(eng_daily_full["day_number"], eng_daily_full["dau_1d"], label="DAU (1d)", alpha=0.8)
ax.plot(eng_daily_full["day_number"], eng_daily_full["wau_7d"], label="WAU (7d)", alpha=0.8)
ax.plot(eng_daily_full["day_number"], eng_daily_full["mau_30d"], label="MAU (30d)", alpha=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel("Day number")
ax.set_ylabel("Users")
ax.set_title("Daily DAU, WAU, MAU")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Chart 5 — DAU/MAU stickiness
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
if "stickiness_series" in dir():
    y_stick = stickiness_series(eng_daily_full)
else:
    y_stick = eng_daily_full["dau_mau_ratio"].fillna(
        eng_daily_full["dau_1d"] / eng_daily_full["mau_30d"]
    )
ax.plot(eng_daily_full["day_number"], y_stick, color="darkgreen")
ax.set_xlabel("Day number")
ax.set_ylabel("DAU / MAU")
ax.set_title("Stickiness (DAU/MAU ratio) over time")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Chart 6 — Event mix
ev = eng_events.sort_values("event_count", ascending=True)
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
plt.barh(ev["event_name"], ev["event_count"], color="coral")
plt.xlabel("Event count")
plt.title("Event mix (total counts in export)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [16]:
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 14 — VISUALIZE: Retention (display in notebook)
# =============================================================================

# Chart 7 — Weekly returning %
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(ret_weekly["week_number"], ret_weekly["returning_pct"] * 100, color="purple", marker="o", ms=3)
ax.set_xlabel("Week number")
ax.set_ylabel("Returning users (%)")
ax.set_title("Weekly share of returning users")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Chart 8 — Cohort Day-1 and Day-7 retention
cohort_plot = ret_cohort_ret.dropna(subset=["day_1_retention_rate"]).copy()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(cohort_plot["cohort_day"], cohort_plot["day_1_retention_rate"], label="Day 1", alpha=0.8)
if "cohort_with_d7" in dir() and cohort_with_d7.shape[0] > 0:
    ax.plot(
        cohort_with_d7["cohort_day"],
        cohort_with_d7["day_7_retention_rate"],
        label="Day 7",
        alpha=0.8,
    )
ax.set_xlabel("Cohort day")
ax.set_ylabel("Retention rate")
ax.set_title("Cohort retention (Day 1 & Day 7)")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Chart 9 — New vs returning (stacked bar by week)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.bar(ret_weekly["week_number"], ret_weekly["returning_users"], label="Returning")
ax.bar(
    ret_weekly["week_number"],
    ret_weekly["new_users"],
    bottom=ret_weekly["returning_users"],
    label="New",
)
ax.set_xlabel("Week number")
ax.set_ylabel("Users")
ax.set_title("Weekly new vs returning users")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 15 — VISUALIZE: User behaviour (display in notebook)
# =============================================================================
# Chart 10 — Top screens by events
top_screens = beh_screens.head(10).sort_values("event_count")
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.barh(top_screens["screen_class"], top_screens["event_count"], color="teal")
plt.xlabel("Event count")
plt.title("Top screens by event count")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
# Chart 11 — Bounce rate on main screens
bounce_df = beh_screens.dropna(subset=["bounce_rate"]).head(8)
if len(bounce_df) > 0:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
    plt.bar(bounce_df["screen_class"], bounce_df["bounce_rate"] * 100, color="salmon")
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha="right")
    plt.ylabel("Bounce rate (%)")
    plt.title("Bounce rate by screen (top 8)")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No bounce_rate data to plot.")
# Chart 12 — Daily returning % from User_Behavior
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(beh_daily_ret["day_number"], beh_daily_ret["returning_pct"] * 100, color="navy")
ax.set_xlabel("Day number")
ax.set_ylabel("Returning (%)")
ax.set_title("Daily returning user share (User_Behavior)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
# Chart 13 — Top 25 cities (optional)
if len(beh_cities_top) > 0:
    ctop = beh_cities_top.sort_values("active_users")
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 7))
    plt.barh(ctop["city"], ctop["active_users"], color="gray")
    plt.xlabel("Active users")
    plt.title("Top 25 cities by active users")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
else:
    print("No city data to plot.")
In [18]:
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 17 — DEEP ANALYSIS: Behaviour health scorecard + funnel + screens
# =============================================================================
# Requires: Sections 0–5, 9 (cohort_with_d7 optional). Uses cleaned CSVs in memory.

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# --- 17.1 Behaviour health scorecard ---
overview_active = pd.to_numeric(beh_overview["active_users"].iloc[0], errors="coerce")
overview_new = pd.to_numeric(beh_overview["new_users"].iloc[0], errors="coerce")
all_users = pd.to_numeric(
    beh_audience.loc[beh_audience["audience"] == "All Users", "active_users"].iloc[0],
    errors="coerce",
)
purchasers = pd.to_numeric(
    beh_audience.loc[beh_audience["audience"] == "Purchasers", "active_users"].iloc[0],
    errors="coerce",
)
purchaser_rate_pct = purchasers / all_users * 100

game_row = beh_screens[beh_screens["screen_class"].str.contains("Game Running", na=False)]
store_row = beh_screens[beh_screens["screen_class"].str.contains("Store|Purchase", na=False, regex=True)]
game_users = pd.to_numeric(game_row["active_users"].iloc[0], errors="coerce") if len(game_row) else np.nan
store_users = pd.to_numeric(store_row["active_users"].iloc[0], errors="coerce") if len(store_row) else np.nan
store_reach_pct = store_users / game_users * 100 if game_users else np.nan

total_events = eng_events["event_count"].sum()
ad_events = eng_events.loc[
    eng_events["event_name"].str.contains("ad_", case=False, na=False), "event_count"
].sum()
ad_event_share_pct = ad_events / total_events * 100

stick = stickiness_series(eng_daily_full) if "stickiness_series" in dir() else (
    eng_daily_full["dau_1d"] / eng_daily_full["mau_30d"]
)
mean_dau_mau = stick.mean()
mean_d7 = ret_cohort_ret["day_7_retention_rate"].dropna().mean() * 100
mean_returning_weekly = ret_weekly["returning_pct"].mean() * 100

us_users = geo.loc[geo["country"] == "United States", "active_users"].sum()
us_share_pct = us_users / geo["active_users"].sum() * 100

new_share_overview = overview_new / overview_active * 100 if overview_active else np.nan

def traffic_light(value, green_ok, amber_ok, higher_is_better=True):
    """Simple RAG for scorecard (green / amber / red)."""
    if pd.isna(value):
        return "N/A"
    if higher_is_better:
        if value >= green_ok:
            return "GREEN"
        if value >= amber_ok:
            return "AMBER"
        return "RED"
    else:
        if value <= green_ok:
            return "GREEN"
        if value <= amber_ok:
            return "AMBER"
        return "RED"

scorecard = pd.DataFrame({
    "Metric": [
        "New users / active users (overview %)",
        "Purchaser rate (%)",
        "Store reach vs Game Running users (%)",
        "Ad event share (%)",
        "Mean Day-7 retention (%)",
        "Mean DAU/MAU (stickiness)",
        "Mean weekly returning (%)",
        "US share of country active users (%)",
    ],
    "Value": [
        round(new_share_overview, 1),
        round(purchaser_rate_pct, 2),
        round(store_reach_pct, 1),
        round(ad_event_share_pct, 1),
        round(mean_d7, 1),
        round(mean_dau_mau, 3),
        round(mean_returning_weekly, 1),
        round(us_share_pct, 1),
    ],
    "Status": [
        traffic_light(new_share_overview, 50, 65, higher_is_better=False),
        traffic_light(purchaser_rate_pct, 2, 1, higher_is_better=True),
        traffic_light(store_reach_pct, 15, 8, higher_is_better=True),
        traffic_light(ad_event_share_pct, 25, 35, higher_is_better=False),
        traffic_light(mean_d7, 15, 10, higher_is_better=True),
        traffic_light(mean_dau_mau, 0.20, 0.12, higher_is_better=True),
        traffic_light(mean_returning_weekly, 75, 65, higher_is_better=True),
        traffic_light(us_share_pct, 50, 65, higher_is_better=False),
    ],
})
print("========== BEHAVIOUR HEALTH SCORECARD ==========")
print(scorecard.to_string(index=False))

# --- 17.2 Master behaviour funnel (event counts + screen users) ---
install_ev = eng_events.loc[eng_events["event_name"] == "first_open_install", "event_count"].sum()
session_ev = eng_events.loc[eng_events["event_name"] == "session_start", "event_count"].sum()
screen_ev = eng_events.loc[eng_events["event_name"] == "screen_view", "event_count"].sum()
engage_ev = eng_events.loc[eng_events["event_name"] == "user_engagement", "event_count"].sum()
iap_ev = eng_events.loc[eng_events["event_name"] == "in_app_purchase", "event_count"].sum()
game_ev = beh_screens.loc[beh_screens["screen_class"] == "Game Running", "event_count"].sum()

funnel = pd.DataFrame({
    "Step": [
        "1 first_open_install (events)",
        "2 session_start (events)",
        "3 screen_view (events)",
        "4 user_engagement (events)",
        "5 Game Running (events)",
        "6 Store/Purchase (users)",
        "7 in_app_purchase (events)",
    ],
    "Count": [install_ev, session_ev, screen_ev, engage_ev, game_ev, store_users, iap_ev],
})
print("\n========== BEHAVIOUR FUNNEL (proxy) ==========")
print(funnel.to_string(index=False))
if install_ev > 0:
    print(f"Install → IAP event ratio: {iap_ev / install_ev * 100:.2f}%")
if game_users > 0:
    print(f"Game users → Store users: {store_reach_pct:.1f}%")

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))
ax.barh(funnel["Step"], funnel["Count"], color="steelblue")
ax.set_xlabel("Count")
ax.set_title("Player behaviour funnel (events + store users)")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# --- 17.3 Screen friction audit ---
scr = beh_screens.copy()
scr["events_per_user"] = scr["event_count"] / scr["active_users"].replace(0, np.nan)
scr["reach"] = scr["active_users"]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 6))
ax.scatter(scr["reach"], scr["events_per_user"], s=80, alpha=0.7)
for _, row in scr.iterrows():
    ax.annotate(row["screen_class"][:20], (row["reach"], row["events_per_user"]), fontsize=7, alpha=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel("Reach (active users)")
ax.set_ylabel("Events per user")
ax.set_title("Screen friction: reach vs intensity")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\n--- Screens: bounce rate (where available) ---")
print(
    scr.dropna(subset=["bounce_rate"])[["screen_class", "bounce_rate", "views_per_user"]]
    .sort_values("bounce_rate", ascending=False)
    .to_string(index=False)
)

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 18 — DEEP ANALYSIS: Segmentation (countries, audiences, markets)
# =============================================================================

# --- 18.1 Country clusters ---
g = geo[~geo["low_sample"]].copy()
med_users = g["active_users"].median()
med_eng = g["engagement_rate"].median()

def country_segment(row):
    big = row["active_users"] >= med_users
    engaged = row["engagement_rate"] >= med_eng
    if big and engaged:
        return "Stars (big + engaged)"
    if big and not engaged:
        return "Big, lower engagement"
    if not big and engaged:
        return "High quality, small"
    return "Small, lower engagement"

g["segment"] = g.apply(country_segment, axis=1)
g["events_per_user"] = g["event_count"] / g["active_users"]
g["key_events_per_user"] = g["key_events"] / g["active_users"]

print("========== COUNTRY SEGMENTS (counts) ==========")
print(g["segment"].value_counts().to_string())

print("\n--- Top 10 engagement_rate (excl. low_sample) ---")
print(g.nlargest(10, "engagement_rate")[["country", "active_users", "engagement_rate", "segment"]].to_string(index=False))

print("\n--- Bottom 10 engagement_rate ---")
print(g.nsmallest(10, "engagement_rate")[["country", "active_users", "engagement_rate", "segment"]].to_string(index=False))

print(f"\nUS concentration: {us_share_pct:.1f}% of country active_users")

print("\n--- High engagement time, smaller markets (outliers) ---")
print(
    g.nlargest(8, "avg_engagement_time_sec")[
        ["country", "active_users", "avg_engagement_time_sec", "total_revenue"]
    ].to_string(index=False)
)

plt.figure(figsize=(9, 6))
for seg, sub in g.groupby("segment"):
    plt.scatter(sub["active_users"], sub["engagement_rate"], label=seg, alpha=0.7, s=60)
plt.xlabel("Active users")
plt.ylabel("Engagement rate")
plt.title("Country segmentation: scale vs engagement quality")
plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.02, 1), loc="upper left")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# --- 18.2 Purchasers vs All Users ---
print("\n========== AUDIENCE: PURCHASERS VS ALL ==========")
print(beh_audience[["audience", "active_users"]].to_string(index=False))
print(f"Purchaser rate: {purchaser_rate_pct:.2f}%")

plt.figure(figsize=(5, 4))
plt.bar(beh_audience["audience"], beh_audience["active_users"], color=["steelblue", "gold"])
plt.ylabel("Active users")
plt.title("Purchasers vs All Users")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

if len(game_row) and len(store_row):
    print(f"Game Running events: {game_row['event_count'].iloc[0]:.0f}")
    print(f"Store/Purchase events: {store_row['event_count'].iloc[0]:.0f}")
    print(f"Game/Store event ratio: {game_row['event_count'].iloc[0] / max(store_row['event_count'].iloc[0], 1):.1f}x")

In [20]:
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 19 — DEEP ANALYSIS: Cross-file links & correlations
# =============================================================================
# Requires: eng_weekly, ret_weekly, eng_daily_full, ret_cohort_ret, ret_cohort_eng,
#           beh_daily_ret, eng_events, ret (full), stickiness_series (Section 3)
#
# Run order: Sections 0 → 5 first, then 17–18, then this cell.

# Create cohort engagement slice if Section 4 did not:
if "ret_cohort_eng" not in dir():
    ret_cohort_eng = ret[ret["table"] == "cohort_day1_7_30_avg_engagement_sec"].copy()

# =============================================================================
# 19.1 Growth vs loyalty (A1) — join on week_number
# =============================================================================
# eng_weekly and ret_weekly BOTH have "new_users" → must use suffixes.

growth_loyalty = eng_weekly.merge(
    ret_weekly[["week_number", "returning_pct", "new_users", "returning_users", "total_users"]],
    on="week_number",
    how="inner",
    suffixes=("_eng", "_ret"),
)

growth_loyalty["new_user_share"] = (
    growth_loyalty["new_users_ret"] / growth_loyalty["total_users"]
)

corr_new_return = growth_loyalty["new_users_ret"].corr(growth_loyalty["returning_pct"])

print("========== A1: GROWTH VS LOYALTY ==========")
print(f"Correlation (new_users_ret vs returning_pct): {corr_new_return:.3f}")
if corr_new_return < -0.2:
    print("→ Weeks with more new users tend to have LOWER returning share (mix shift).")
elif corr_new_return > 0.2:
    print("→ New users and returning share move together (check GA definitions).")
else:
    print("→ Weak linear relationship; inspect specific weeks.")

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 4))
ax1.plot(
    growth_loyalty["week_number"],
    growth_loyalty["active_users"],
    color="steelblue",
    label="Active users (eng)",
)
ax1.set_xlabel("Week number")
ax1.set_ylabel("Active users", color="steelblue")
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
ax2.plot(
    growth_loyalty["week_number"],
    growth_loyalty["returning_pct"] * 100,
    color="purple",
)
ax2.set_ylabel("Returning users (%)", color="purple")
ax1.set_title("Growth vs loyalty by week")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

heavy_new = growth_loyalty[growth_loyalty["new_user_share"] > 0.5][
    ["week_number", "new_users_ret", "total_users", "new_user_share", "returning_pct"]
]
print(f"\nWeeks with >50% new users: {len(heavy_new)}")
if len(heavy_new) > 0:
    print(heavy_new.head(10).to_string(index=False))

# =============================================================================
# 19.2 Engagement depth vs retention (A2) — cohort_day
# =============================================================================
# Both cohort tables share the SAME wide header → merge ONLY needed columns
# (avoids day_1_retention_rate → _ret / _eng KeyError).

ret_cols = ["cohort_day", "day_1_retention_rate", "day_7_retention_rate", "day_30_retention_rate"]
eng_cols = ["cohort_day", "day_1_avg_engagement_sec", "day_7_avg_engagement_sec", "day_30_avg_engagement_sec"]

cohort_merged = ret_cohort_ret[ret_cols].merge(
    ret_cohort_eng[eng_cols],
    on="cohort_day",
    how="inner",
)

d1 = cohort_merged.dropna(subset=["day_1_retention_rate", "day_1_avg_engagement_sec"])
d7 = cohort_merged.dropna(subset=["day_7_retention_rate", "day_7_avg_engagement_sec"])

print("\n========== A2: ENGAGEMENT DEPTH VS RETENTION ==========")
if len(d1) > 2:
    r1 = d1["day_1_retention_rate"].corr(d1["day_1_avg_engagement_sec"])
    print(f"Corr(Day-1 retention, Day-1 avg engagement sec): {r1:.3f}")
if len(d7) > 2:
    r7 = d7["day_7_retention_rate"].corr(d7["day_7_avg_engagement_sec"])
    print(f"Corr(Day-7 retention, Day-7 avg engagement sec): {r7:.3f}")

if len(d1) > 0:
    plt.figure(figsize=(7, 5))
    plt.scatter(d1["day_1_avg_engagement_sec"], d1["day_1_retention_rate"], alpha=0.5)
    plt.xlabel("Day-1 avg engagement (sec)")
    plt.ylabel("Day-1 retention rate")
    plt.title("Cohort Day-1: engagement vs retention")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

c7 = cohort_merged.dropna(subset=["day_7_retention_rate"])
if len(c7) > 0:
    print("\nBest cohort days (Day-7 retention):")
    print(
        c7.nlargest(5, "day_7_retention_rate")[["cohort_day", "day_7_retention_rate"]].to_string(
            index=False
        )
    )
    print("\nWorst cohort days (Day-7 retention):")
    print(
        c7.nsmallest(5, "day_7_retention_rate")[["cohort_day", "day_7_retention_rate"]].to_string(
            index=False
        )
    )

# =============================================================================
# 19.3 Stickiness trend (early vs late year)
# =============================================================================
if "stickiness_series" in dir():
    stick_full = stickiness_series(eng_daily_full)
else:
    stick_full = (
        pd.to_numeric(eng_daily_full["dau_1d"], errors="coerce")
        / pd.to_numeric(eng_daily_full["mau_30d"], errors="coerce")
    )

first30 = stick_full.iloc[:30].mean()
last30 = stick_full.iloc[-30:].mean()
print("\n========== STICKINESS TREND ==========")
print(f"Mean DAU/MAU days 0–29:    {first30:.3f}")
print(f"Mean DAU/MAU last 30 days: {last30:.3f}")
print("Trend:", "improving" if last30 > first30 else "declining or flat")

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
plt.plot(eng_daily_full["day_number"], stick_full, color="darkgreen")
plt.xlabel("Day number")
plt.ylabel("DAU/MAU")
plt.title("Stickiness over 2025")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# =============================================================================
# 19.4 Peak / trough weeks
# =============================================================================
peak_w = eng_weekly.loc[eng_weekly["active_users"].idxmax()]
trough_w = eng_weekly.loc[eng_weekly["active_users"].idxmin()]
print("\n========== ENGAGEMENT PEAK/TROUGH WEEKS ==========")
print(f"Peak week {int(peak_w['week_number'])}: active_users={peak_w['active_users']:.0f}")
print(f"Trough week {int(trough_w['week_number'])}: active_users={trough_w['active_users']:.0f}")

# =============================================================================
# 19.5 Quarterly returning %
# =============================================================================
rw = ret_weekly.copy()
rw["quarter"] = pd.cut(
    rw["week_number"].astype(int),
    bins=[-1, 12, 25, 38, 52],
    labels=["Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"],
)
print("\n========== MEAN RETURNING % BY QUARTER ==========")
print((rw.groupby("quarter", observed=True)["returning_pct"].mean() * 100).round(1).to_string())

# =============================================================================
# 19.6 Recent cohort (Nov–Dec window if present)
# =============================================================================
nov_cohort = ret[ret["table"] == "cohort_retention_rate"].copy()
if len(nov_cohort) > 0:
    nov_cohort["cohort_day"] = pd.to_numeric(nov_cohort["cohort_day"], errors="coerce")
    rate_col = "retention_rate" if "retention_rate" in nov_cohort.columns else "retention_pct"
    nov_cohort[rate_col] = pd.to_numeric(nov_cohort[rate_col], errors="coerce")
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
    plt.plot(nov_cohort["cohort_day"], nov_cohort[rate_col], marker="o", ms=3)
    plt.xlabel("Cohort day")
    plt.ylabel("Retention rate")
    plt.title("Recent install cohort retention (Nov–Dec export)")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    print("\nRecent cohort plotted — check for steep early drop-off.")
else:
    print("\nNo cohort_retention_rate table in Retention.csv.")

# =============================================================================
# 19.7 Daily returning curve (User_Behavior)
# =============================================================================
dr = beh_daily_ret.copy()
dr["rolling_returning_pct"] = dr["returning_pct"].rolling(7, min_periods=1).mean()

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4))
plt.plot(dr["day_number"], dr["returning_pct"] * 100, alpha=0.35, label="Daily")
plt.plot(dr["day_number"], dr["rolling_returning_pct"] * 100, label="7-day rolling mean")
plt.xlabel("Day number")
plt.ylabel("Returning %")
plt.title("Daily returning share — when do users drop off?")
plt.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print("\nSection 19 complete. Run Section 20 next (uses corr_new_return from 19.1).")

========== A1: GROWTH VS LOYALTY ==========
Correlation (new_users_ret vs returning_pct): -0.750
→ Weeks with more new users tend to have LOWER returning share (mix shift).

Weeks with >50% new users: 0

========== A2: ENGAGEMENT DEPTH VS RETENTION ==========
Corr(Day-1 retention, Day-1 avg engagement sec): 0.099
Corr(Day-7 retention, Day-7 avg engagement sec): -0.055

Best cohort days (Day-7 retention):
 cohort_day  day_7_retention_rate
        209              1.000000
        245              1.000000
        315              1.000000
        190              0.666667
         13              0.500000

Worst cohort days (Day-7 retention):
 cohort_day  day_7_retention_rate
         18              0.100000
         11              0.111111
         10              0.125000
         23              0.142857
         38              0.142857

========== STICKINESS TREND ==========
Mean DAU/MAU days 0–29:    0.071
Mean DAU/MAU last 30 days: 0.055
Trend: declining or flat

========== ENGAGEMENT PEAK/TROUGH WEEKS ==========
Peak week 13: active_users=132
Trough week 0: active_users=37

========== MEAN RETURNING % BY QUARTER ==========
quarter
Q1    74.9
Q2    77.2
Q3    76.6
Q4    76.0

Recent cohort plotted — check for steep early drop-off.

Section 19 complete. Run Section 20 next (uses corr_new_return from 19.1).
In [22]:
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 20 — SYNTHESIS: Issue → Evidence → Action + executive conclusion
# =============================================================================
# Requires: Sections 0–5, 17–19 (or recomputes core metrics below).

# --- 20.0 Recompute key metrics if Section 17 was skipped ---
if "purchaser_rate_pct" not in dir():
    all_users = pd.to_numeric(
        beh_audience.loc[beh_audience["audience"] == "All Users", "active_users"].iloc[0],
        errors="coerce",
    )
    purchasers = pd.to_numeric(
        beh_audience.loc[beh_audience["audience"] == "Purchasers", "active_users"].iloc[0],
        errors="coerce",
    )
    purchaser_rate_pct = purchasers / all_users * 100

    game_row = beh_screens[beh_screens["screen_class"].str.contains("Game Running", na=False)]
    store_row = beh_screens[beh_screens["screen_class"].str.contains("Store|Purchase", na=False, regex=True)]
    game_users = pd.to_numeric(game_row["active_users"].iloc[0], errors="coerce") if len(game_row) else np.nan
    store_users = pd.to_numeric(store_row["active_users"].iloc[0], errors="coerce") if len(store_row) else np.nan
    store_reach_pct = store_users / game_users * 100 if game_users else np.nan

    total_events = eng_events["event_count"].sum()
    ad_events = eng_events.loc[
        eng_events["event_name"].str.contains("ad_", case=False, na=False), "event_count"
    ].sum()
    ad_event_share_pct = ad_events / total_events * 100

    if "stickiness_series" in dir():
        mean_dau_mau = stickiness_series(eng_daily_full).mean()
    else:
        mean_dau_mau = (eng_daily_full["dau_1d"] / eng_daily_full["mau_30d"]).mean()

    mean_d7 = ret_cohort_ret["day_7_retention_rate"].dropna().mean() * 100
    mean_returning_weekly = ret_weekly["returning_pct"].mean() * 100
    us_share_pct = (
        geo.loc[geo["country"] == "United States", "active_users"].sum()
        / geo["active_users"].sum()
        * 100
    )
    overview_active = pd.to_numeric(beh_overview["active_users"].iloc[0], errors="coerce")
    overview_new = pd.to_numeric(beh_overview["new_users"].iloc[0], errors="coerce")

# Correlation from Section 19 (fallback if 19.1 not run)
if "corr_new_return" not in dir():
    gl = eng_weekly.merge(
        ret_weekly[["week_number", "returning_pct", "new_users", "total_users"]],
        on="week_number",
        how="inner",
        suffixes=("_eng", "_ret"),
    )
    corr_new_return = gl["new_users_ret"].corr(gl["returning_pct"])

# Traffic source text
traffic_note = "Direct / (none) dominates"
if "beh_traffic" in dir() and len(beh_traffic) > 0:
    sm = beh_traffic["source_medium"].dropna()
    if len(sm) > 0:
        traffic_note = str(sm.iloc[0])

# --- 20.1 Issue → Action matrix ---
issues_actions = pd.DataFrame({
    "Issue": [
        "Monetization funnel leak",
        "Tiny payer base",
        "Ad-heavy product loop",
        "Early retention cliff",
        "Geographic concentration",
        "Single acquisition channel",
    ],
    "Evidence": [
        f"Store users {store_users:.0f} vs Game Running {game_users:.0f} ({store_reach_pct:.1f}% reach)",
        f"Purchasers {purchasers:.0f} / All users {all_users:.0f} ({purchaser_rate_pct:.2f}%)",
        f"Ad events {ad_event_share_pct:.1f}% of total; Fullscreen Ad high volume in screens",
        f"Mean Day-7 retention {mean_d7:.1f}%; daily returning curve drops after early days",
        f"United States ~{us_share_pct:.1f}% of country active users",
        f"Traffic source: {traffic_note}",
    ],
    "Hypothesis": [
        "Players engage with the game but rarely reach or convert in the store",
        "IAP audience is not scaling with the active user base",
        "Ad exposure may compete with core loop depth and session quality",
        "Onboarding and Day 0–7 experience weak relative to loyal returning base",
        "Growth and product decisions over-optimized for one market",
        "Limited channel mix restricts install quality and diversification",
    ],
    "Recommended action": [
        "Add store CTA after engagement milestones; simplify Store/Purchase flow",
        "Test first-purchase offer and bundles for engaged non-payers",
        "Cap interstitial frequency; improve return path to Game Running after ads",
        "Strengthen Day-0 tutorial, push/rewards for D1 and D7 return",
        "Run Tier-2 tests in high-engagement smaller countries before scaling UA",
        "Pilot one non-Direct acquisition channel; track D7 by source",
    ],
    "Owner": [
        "Product",
        "Monetization",
        "Product",
        "Product / CRM",
        "Marketing",
        "Marketing",
    ],
})

print("========== ISSUE → ACTION MATRIX  ==========\n")
for i, row in issues_actions.iterrows():
    print(f"{i+1}. Issue: {row['Issue']}")
    print(f"   Evidence: {row['Evidence']}")
    print(f"   Hypothesis: {row['Hypothesis']}")
    print(f"   Recommended action: {row['Recommended action']}")
    print(f"   Owner: {row['Owner']}\n")

# --- 20.2 Deep KPI summary ---
top_country = geo.loc[geo["active_users"].idxmax(), "country"]
top_screen = beh_screens.sort_values("event_count", ascending=False).iloc[0]["screen_class"]

kpi_deep = pd.DataFrame({
    "KPI": [
        "Total active users (overview)",
        "Total new users (overview)",
        "New / active ratio (overview %)",
        "Purchaser rate (%)",
        "Store reach / Game users (%)",
        "Ad event share (%)",
        "Mean DAU/MAU (stickiness)",
        "Mean weekly returning (%)",
        "Mean Day-7 retention (%)",
        "US share of active users (%)",
        "Corr(new_users, returning_pct) weekly",
        "Top country",
        "Top screen (by events)",
    ],
    "Value": [
        round(overview_active, 0),
        round(overview_new, 0),
        round(overview_new / overview_active * 100, 1) if overview_active else np.nan,
        round(purchaser_rate_pct, 2),
        round(store_reach_pct, 1),
        round(ad_event_share_pct, 1),
        round(mean_dau_mau, 3),
        round(mean_returning_weekly, 1),
        round(mean_d7, 1),
        round(us_share_pct, 1),
        round(corr_new_return, 3),
        top_country,
        top_screen,
    ],
})

print("\n========== DEEP KPI SUMMARY (FOR REPORT SLIDE) ==========")
print(kpi_deep.to_string(index=False))

# --- 20.3 Optional: save tables for Word/PowerPoint ---
try:
    issues_actions.to_csv(DATA_DIR / "issue_action_matrix.csv", index=False)
    kpi_deep.to_csv(DATA_DIR / "kpi_deep_summary.csv", index=False)
    print("\nSaved: issue_action_matrix.csv, kpi_deep_summary.csv")
except NameError:
    print("\n(DATA_DIR not defined — skip CSV export or set DATA_DIR in Section 0)")

# --- 20.4 Executive conclusion (for written report) ---
print("""
========== EXECUTIVE CONCLUSION  ==========

1. SCALE & LOYALTY
   The game maintains a meaningful returning user base (high weekly returning %),
   but growth in new users does not always align with loyalty (see weekly correlation).
   Action: Balance user acquisition with retention campaigns.

2. MONETIZATION GAP
   Very few users reach the store or become purchasers relative to Game Running reach.
   Action: Improve game-to-store funnel and first-purchase conversion.

3. PRODUCT MIX
   Ad-related events and screens are a large share of behaviour versus IAP signals.
   Action: Optimize ad load vs session quality; do not scale UA without store fixes.

4. RETENTION
   Day-7 cohort retention and early-day returning curves show opportunity in the first week.
   Action: Invest in Day-0–7 onboarding and re-engagement.

5. MARKETS & ACQUISITION
   Users are concentrated in the United States with mostly Direct traffic.
   Action: Diversify channels and test high-engagement smaller countries.

6. ANALYTICS MATURITY
   This four-CSV BI pipeline turns Firebase aggregates into actionable dashboards.
   Next step: user-level BigQuery data for true funnels, segments, and churn models.

========== LIMITATIONS ==========
• Aggregate exports only (no user_id).
• Funnel metrics are proxy counts, not user conversion rates.
• Day-30 retention largely sparse; D1/D7 and weekly metrics used instead.
• iOS-heavy, Direct-dominated traffic in current export.

========== END OF ANALYSIS ==========
""")

========== ISSUE → ACTION MATRIX (ALA GAMES) ==========

1. Issue: Monetization funnel leak
   Evidence: Store users 90 vs Game Running 1812 (5.0% reach)
   Hypothesis: Players engage with the game but rarely reach or convert in the store
   Recommended action: Add store CTA after engagement milestones; simplify Store/Purchase flow
   Owner: Product

2. Issue: Tiny payer base
   Evidence: Purchasers 16 / All users 1825 (0.88%)
   Hypothesis: IAP audience is not scaling with the active user base
   Recommended action: Test first-purchase offer and bundles for engaged non-payers
   Owner: Monetization

3. Issue: Ad-heavy product loop
   Evidence: Ad events 22.2% of total; Fullscreen Ad high volume in screens
   Hypothesis: Ad exposure may compete with core loop depth and session quality
   Recommended action: Cap interstitial frequency; improve return path to Game Running after ads
   Owner: Product

4. Issue: Early retention cliff
   Evidence: Mean Day-7 retention 30.8%; daily returning curve drops after early days
   Hypothesis: Onboarding and Day 0–7 experience weak relative to loyal returning base
   Recommended action: Strengthen Day-0 tutorial, push/rewards for D1 and D7 return
   Owner: Product / CRM

5. Issue: Geographic concentration
   Evidence: United States ~58.7% of country active users
   Hypothesis: Growth and product decisions over-optimized for one market
   Recommended action: Run Tier-2 tests in high-engagement smaller countries before scaling UA
   Owner: Marketing

6. Issue: Single acquisition channel
   Evidence: Traffic source: (direct) / (none)
   Hypothesis: Limited channel mix restricts install quality and diversification
   Recommended action: Pilot one non-Direct acquisition channel; track D7 by source
   Owner: Marketing


========== DEEP KPI SUMMARY (FOR REPORT SLIDE) ==========
                                  KPI         Value
        Total active users (overview)        1825.0
           Total new users (overview)        1271.0
      New / active ratio (overview %)          69.6
                   Purchaser rate (%)          0.88
         Store reach / Game users (%)           5.0
                   Ad event share (%)          22.2
            Mean DAU/MAU (stickiness)         0.063
            Mean weekly returning (%)          76.2
             Mean Day-7 retention (%)          30.8
         US share of active users (%)          58.7
Corr(new_users, returning_pct) weekly         -0.75
                          Top country United States
               Top screen (by events)  Game Running

Saved: issue_action_matrix.csv, kpi_deep_summary.csv

========== EXECUTIVE CONCLUSION (ALA GAMES) ==========

1. SCALE & LOYALTY
   The game maintains a meaningful returning user base (high weekly returning %),
   but growth in new users does not always align with loyalty (see weekly correlation).
   Action: Balance user acquisition with retention campaigns.

2. MONETIZATION GAP
   Very few users reach the store or become purchasers relative to Game Running reach.
   Action: Improve game-to-store funnel and first-purchase conversion.

3. PRODUCT MIX
   Ad-related events and screens are a large share of behaviour versus IAP signals.
   Action: Optimize ad load vs session quality; do not scale UA without store fixes.

4. RETENTION
   Day-7 cohort retention and early-day returning curves show opportunity in the first week.
   Action: Invest in Day-0–7 onboarding and re-engagement.

5. MARKETS & ACQUISITION
   Users are concentrated in the United States with mostly Direct traffic.
   Action: Diversify channels and test high-engagement smaller countries.

6. ANALYTICS MATURITY
   This four-CSV BI pipeline turns Firebase aggregates into actionable dashboards.
   Next step: user-level BigQuery data for true funnels, segments, and churn models.

========== LIMITATIONS ==========
• Aggregate exports only (no user_id).
• Funnel metrics are proxy counts, not user conversion rates.
• Day-30 retention largely sparse; D1/D7 and weekly metrics used instead.
• iOS-heavy, Direct-dominated traffic in current export.

========== END OF ANALYSIS ==========
